description_long: This object is a roughly rectangular stone block with a weathered, uneven surface and prominent carved details on one face. The front features two cylindrical columns with stepped bases, flanking a recessed central area that may have once held an inscription or decorative element. The stone's surface is pitted and irregular, with visible signs of erosion and chipping, especially along the top and corners. The sides and back are less detailed, with the back being mostly flat and unadorned, while the sides show some shallow recesses and rough texturing. The coloration is a mix of light beige and off-white, with darker brownish stains in crevices and on the upper surfaces. This block appears to be a fragment of architectural ornamentation, possibly from an ancient or classical structure.
description: A weathered, beige stone block with carved columns and a recessed central panel on one face, showing signs of age and erosion.
description_view_0: The front view displays two carved columns with stepped bases and a recessed central area, highlighting the most ornate features.
description_view_1: The left side view reveals a rough, irregular surface with shallow recesses and less detail than the front.
description_view_2: The back view is mostly flat and undecorated, showing the block's plain, weathered stone surface.
description_view_3: The right side view mirrors the left, with visible column details wrapping slightly around the edge and a rough, pitted texture.
attribute_location_context: ancient temple foundation or archaeological site
attribute_installation_method: dry-stacked or set on a stone base
attribute_weathering_pattern: The stone shows significant surface erosion, pitting, and chipping, especially on the top and corners, with some darker staining in crevices.
